description: Fix cross-profile dispatch routing for multi-connection WebSocket support. When two browser profiles are connected, browser tools without a tabId (browser_open_tab, browser_list_tabs, extension_get_state, etc.) route to an arbitrary connection via getAnyConnection(), causing the AI to see the wrong profile's tabs and open tabs in the wrong browser. The fix makes browser_list_tabs broadcast to all connections and merge results, gives action tools like browser_open_tab a connectionId parameter, includes connectionId on plugin_list_tabs results so the AI can identify which profile owns each tab, and fixes network capture state tracking to use the correct connection.

userStories.title: Make browser_list_tabs broadcast to all connections and merge results
userStories.description: browser_list_tabs currently dispatches to one arbitrary connection via resolveConnection, so the AI only sees one profile's tabs. Change it to dispatch to ALL connections, merge the results, and annotate each tab with its connectionId so the AI can distinguish profiles.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_list_tabs returns tabs from ALL connected profiles, not just one
Each tab in the response includes a connectionId field identifying which profile it belongs to
When only one connection exists, behavior is identical to before (just adds connectionId)
When no connections exist, returns an error 'Extension not connected'

userStories.title: Add connectionId parameter to browser_open_tab and other action tools
userStories.description: browser_open_tab dispatches with no tabId, so resolveConnection picks an arbitrary connection. Add an optional connectionId parameter to browser_open_tab so the AI can target the correct profile. Apply the same pattern to extension_get_state and extension_get_logs which also have no tabId.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_open_tab accepts an optional connectionId parameter
When connectionId is provided, the dispatch routes to that specific connection
When connectionId is not provided and only one connection exists, it routes there (backwards compat)
When connectionId is not provided and multiple connections exist, it routes to the first connection (unchanged fallback)
extension_get_state and extension_get_logs accept an optional connectionId parameter
resolveConnection considers params.connectionId in addition to params.tabId

userStories.title: Include connectionId in plugin_list_tabs results and tab state
userStories.description: plugin_list_tabs returns merged tab data from all connections but doesn't tell the AI which connection owns each tab. Add connectionId to each tab entry so the AI can use it for targeted dispatch.
acceptanceCriteria: Each tab entry in plugin_list_tabs results includes a connectionId field
The connectionId matches the ExtensionConnection.connectionId that reported the tab
getMergedTabMapping or the plugin_list_tabs handler annotates tabs with their source connection

userStories.title: Fix network capture state tracking to use the correct connection
userStories.description: enable_network_capture and disable_network_capture use getAnyConnection(state) to track active captures, which may update the wrong connection's activeNetworkCaptures set. Use getConnectionForTab to find the correct connection.
acceptanceCriteria: enable_network_capture tracks the capture on the connection that owns the tab
disable_network_capture removes the capture from the connection that owns the tab
When the tab's connection can't be found, falls back to getAnyConnection (graceful degradation)

userStories.title: Update unit tests for routing fixes
userStories.description: Update and add unit tests to verify the corrected routing logic: resolveConnection with connectionId, browser_list_tabs broadcast, plugin_list_tabs connectionId annotations, and network capture tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests verify resolveConnection prefers connectionId over tabId over getAnyConnection
Tests verify browser_list_tabs merges results from multiple connections with connectionId
Tests verify plugin_list_tabs tabs include connectionId
Tests verify enable/disable_network_capture tracks on the correct connection
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.test.ts (add resolveConnection tests for connectionId)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/list-tabs.test.ts or inline tests (new — test broadcast behavior)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/plugin-list-tabs.test.ts or inline (test connectionId annotation)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/enable-network-capture.test.ts or existing test file

Context:
The existing extension-protocol.test.ts already has tests for dispatchToExtension and resolveConnection with tabId-based routing. Add new test cases for connectionId routing.

New test cases:
1. resolveConnection with connectionId in params returns the matching connection
2. resolveConnection with connectionId takes priority over tabId when both are present
3. resolveConnection with unknown connectionId falls back to tabId routing
4. resolveConnection with unknown connectionId and no tabId falls back to getAnyConnection
5. dispatchToAllConnections dispatches to all connections and merges results
6. dispatchToAllConnections handles partial failures (one connection errors, another succeeds)
7. browser_list_tabs handler returns tabs from all connections with connectionId
8. plugin_list_tabs handler returns tabs with connectionId annotations
9. enable_network_capture uses getConnectionForTab for tracking

Constraints:
- Follow existing test patterns — use vi.fn() mocks for WsHandle, create mock ExtensionConnections with state.extensionConnections.set()
- Do NOT modify unrelated tests

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for multi-connection routing
userStories.description: Add E2E tests that verify browser_list_tabs returns tabs from all connections, browser_open_tab with connectionId routes correctly, and plugin_list_tabs includes connectionId annotations.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: browser_list_tabs with two connections returns tabs from both, each annotated with connectionId
Test: browser_open_tab with connectionId routes to the specified connection
Test: plugin_list_tabs with two connections returns tabs with connectionId field
Test: network capture on a specific tab tracks on the correct connection
All existing E2E tests pass (no regressions)
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-connection.e2e.ts (add new tests to existing file, or create if not merged yet)
- e2e/fixtures.ts (use existing createRawWsConnection helper or fetchWsInfo pattern)

Context:
The multi-connection E2E tests from the previous PRD (prd-multi-ws-connection) should already exist. Add routing-specific tests.

The pattern for creating a raw WS connection from dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts:
```typescript
const { wsUrl, wsSecret } = await fetchWsInfo(mcpServer.port, mcpServer.secret);
const protocols = ['opentabs'];
if (wsSecret) protocols.push(wsSecret);
protocols.push('conn-beta'); // connectionId
const wsBeta = new WebSocket(wsUrl, protocols);
await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
  wsBeta.onopen = () => resolve();
  wsBeta.onerror = () => reject();
});
```

New test cases:

1. **browser_list_tabs returns merged tabs from both connections**:
   - Standard fixture gives us the real extension (connection 'alpha')
   - Create a raw WS connection with connectionId 'beta'
   - Send a fake tab.syncAll from 'beta' with a tab entry
   - Call browser_list_tabs via MCP client
   - Verify results include tabs from both the real extension AND the fake connection
   - Each tab has a connectionId field

2. **browser_open_tab with connectionId targets the correct connection**:
   - Standard fixture + second raw WS connection
   - Call browser_open_tab with `{ url: '...', connectionId: '<real-extension-connectionId>' }`
   - Verify the tab opens in the real extension (not the fake WS)
   - Read the connectionId from browser_list_tabs to know the real extension's ID

3. **plugin_list_tabs includes connectionId**:
   - Standard fixture + second raw WS connection with tab.syncAll
   - Call plugin_list_tabs
   - Verify each tab entry has a connectionId field

Constraints:
- The raw WS connection can respond to dispatches if needed (it receives browser.listTabs dispatch as a JSON-RPC message). For the merge test, the fake connection needs to handle the dispatch and send back a response with fake tab data.
- Use waitForLog to ensure connections are established before dispatching.
- Each test must be fully isolated — close all WebSocket connections in finally blocks.
